---
title: "GitHub Workflow"
author: "S.C. McClelland"
date: "2026-02-24"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Overview

Users should follow the guidelines in this document for creating branches from main to add features or fix scripts.

## Best practices

-   Never commit directly to main.

-   Always checkout main at beginning of a session.

-   One branch per task.

-   Push branch, open PR early, update PR with more commits.

-   Merge main into your branch regularly (git merge main).

-   After PR merges, delete branch and pull main.

## Conventions

branches: - feature/<initials>-<short-desc>

-   fix/<initials>-<short-desc>

-   docs/<short-desc>

commits: - start with a verb (Add, Fix, Refactor, Document)

-   keep short (a few words only)

### New users (one-time set-up)

```{bash one time set-up}
# (1) clone
git clone <repo_url>
cd <repo_folder>
#
# (2) identity
git config --global user.name "First Last"
git config --global user.email "email@domain.edu"
#
# (3) confirm remotes
git remote -v
#
# (4) authentication
# use SSH keys (recommended)
```

### User workflow

```{bash workflow}
# (1) start task
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git checkout -b feature/<initials>-short-desc
# Examples: 
# feature/SCM-res-histogram
# feature/SCM-ccg-res-percentiles
#
# (2) work loop
# edit --> status --> add --> commit
git status
git add <file1> <file2>
git commit -m "Short, specific message"
#
# (3) push branch and open pull request (PR)
#
# push
git push -u origin feature/<initials>-short-desc
#
# then open PR using the web UI
# go to "Pull requests," open, and assign to PI for review
# review and address any comments, continue to push to the same branch
# if building out the same feature
```

### Avoiding conflicts

```{bash conflicts}
# at load, set user name and email
git config user.name "Your Name"
git config user.email "your_email@example.com"

# stay up-to-date with main
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git checkout feature/<initials>-short-desc
git merge main
# If conflict occurs
# Option 1: --ours or --theirs
# works well if one version is clearly correct and you don't need to combine changes
# keep your version (if that is correct option)
git checkout --ours <file>
git add <file>
# keep main's version (if that is correct option)
git checkout --theirs <file>
git add <file>
# Option 2:
code .
# Open conflicted files, using VS code, resolve in an editor
# However, to use on HPC requires specific set-up
# Should offer easy options using buttons at top
# One time set-up
git config --global core.editor "code --wait"
git config --global merge.tool vscode
# Refer to Google doc for set-up process, need SSH key
# Then:
git add <resolved_files>
git commit -m "Resolve merge conflicts with main"
git push
```

### Update local after branch merge to main

```{bash update}
git checkout main
git pull origin main
# delete local 
git branch -d feature/<initials>-short-desc
# prune deleted remote branch
git fetch --prune
```
